rules.description: Produce the most natural-sounding translation avoiding literal transposition of English syntax
rules.notes: Sentences need to be rewritten with adapted syntax. Balance faithfulness to source and readability in target language.

rules.description: Use clear and precise style that should not read like a translation
rules.notes: Translation should be easily understood by native speakers at all times

rules.description: Avoid heaviness, ambiguities, repetitions and Anglicisms
rules.notes: Also avoid enriching the text excessively

rules.description: Translate buttons as verbs, generally using infinitive form
rules.notes: Be concise, avoid articles or prepositions. Pay attention to character/space limitations.

rules.description: Button translations should not end with period unless they include more than one sentence

rules.section: Names of RingCentral Products, Applications and Features
rules.description: Check reference material and linguistic instructions including terminology and DNT lists before translating product/application names
rules.notes: Many product/application names are trademarked and not localizable

rules.description: Replace English examples with Spanish equivalents when needed for localization
rules.notes: Example given about wildcards explanation needing adaptation

rules.description: Keep professional and welcoming tone without being patronizing or pompous
rules.notes: Always keep in mind the user/target audience

rules.description: Be precise, clear, consistent and natural. Rephrase unclear sentences
rules.notes: Break up long English sentences into smaller parts for better understanding

rules.description: Focus on usability with accurate and concise translations
rules.notes: No ambiguities or mistranslations

rules.description: Be clear and coherent so target reader understands easily
rules.notes: Text should be readable

rules.description: Be objective and neutral without inserting personal opinion
rules.notes: Avoid colloquial or slang words, be politically correct and aim for bias-free language

rules.description: Content and numbers should be free of mistakes
rules.notes: Ensure correctness

rules.description: Use idiomatically correct verbs, metaphors, expressions so text doesn't sound like translation
Be complete - do not leave out content
rules.notes: Don't omit words, phrases, or whole paragraphs

rules.description: Keep an appropriate business-like tone for the target reader
Ensure consistency within and across files using RingCentral approved tools and documents
rules.notes: Consistency is of utmost importance

rules.description: Always check RingCentral term lists as first and binding source for translation
rules.notes: Use most recent version as they are frequently updated

rules.description: For other terms consult Microsoft Language Portal and IT vendor portals
rules.notes: Available at https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/language

rules.description: Translate internal cross-references consistently throughout translation and all files
rules.notes: Internal cross-references mention other parts of text within the file/document being translated

rules.description: For external cross-references, check if document was localized before and match translations
rules.notes: If document not translated before, leave as in source language

rules.description: Use formal addressing 'usted' not 'tu' or 'vos'
rules.example_incorrect: Por favor, introduce tu nombre de usuario
rules.notes: English tends to be over polite. It's not necessary to use 'Please' with user instructions.

rules.description: Be more direct and use imperative form where appropriate
rules.example_incorrect: Por favor, introduce tu nombre de usuario

rules.description: Spanish gives priority to masculine forms unless context requires otherwise
rules.example_correct: Los usuarios pueden contactar con la asistencia al cliente cuando necesiten ayuda.
rules.example_incorrect: Los usuarios y las usuarias pueden contactar con la asistencia al cliente cuando necesiten ayuda.
rules.notes: Try to find neutral forms when possible. Balance bias-free language, fluency and character limitations.

rules.description: Use neutral welcome forms instead of gendered ones
Avoid passive voice when possible, use active form or reflexive passive voice with 'se'
rules.example_correct: Se han facilitado todos los detalles sobre este tema.
rules.example_incorrect: Todos los detalles sobre este tema han sido facilitados.
rules.notes: Passive voice not as common as in English and sounds wordy. Exception: legal/official documents.

rules.description: Use active voice to improve readability
rules.example_correct: En este informe técnico aparecen algunas funciones nuevas.
rules.example_incorrect: En este informe técnico son detalladas algunas funciones nuevas.

rules.description: Avoid reproducing English negative constructions when they sound unnatural
rules.example_correct: Solo desmarque la casilla si es obligatorio
rules.example_incorrect: No desmarque la casilla si no es obligatorio
rules.notes: Positive constructions may be better, but check to avoid distortions from source

rules.description: Don't transfer extensive use of English possessive adjectives to Spanish
rules.example_correct: Puede editar los archivos desde una computadora o desde un celular
rules.example_incorrect: Puede editar sus archivos desde su computadora o desde su celular
rules.notes: Simple articles can often replace possessive adjectives. 'Our' sometimes refers to third person.

rules.description: Follow standard Spanish grammar rules for proper use of articles
Don't always translate -ing form as gerund; consider context and use appropriate form
rules.example_correct: Nuestros controladores disponen de una memoria que protege la integridad de sus datos.
rules.example_incorrect: Nuestros controladores disponen de una memoria protegiendo la integridad de sus datos.
rules.notes: Gerund marks action in progress before/during main action. Not correct for actions after/consequence of another or as adjective.

rules.description: Use conditional construction instead of gerund for conditional actions
rules.example_correct: Si accede al sitio web, acepta los términos y condiciones.
rules.example_incorrect: Accediendo al sitio web, acepta los términos y condiciones.

rules.description: Translate gerunds in English headings/titles as noun forms, not gerunds
rules.notes: Use verbal forms consistently as alternative

rules.description: Use present tense for product introductions instead of gerund
Check context and concentrate on meaning, render as clearly as possible
rules.example_incorrect: Introduzca su nombre, su dirección de correo electrónico y su fecha de nacimiento.
rules.notes: English sometimes uses additional words to express concepts

rules.description: Simplify temporal expressions for clarity
rules.example_correct: Ahora puede acceder a todo el contenido.
rules.example_incorrect: Esto significa que a partir de ahora puede acceder a todo el contenido.

rules.description: Use pronouns or other techniques to avoid repetitions that are acceptable in English but not Spanish
rules.example_correct: Para eliminar un archivo de su computadora, selecciónelo en el panel derecho y haga clic en Suprimir.
rules.example_incorrect: Para eliminar un archivo de su ordenador, seleccione el archivo en el panel derecho y haga clic en Suprimir.

rules.description: Do not use 'mismo/misma' as pronouns
rules.notes: Using the adjective 'mismo/misma' as pronouns is incorrect

rules.description: Be careful with false friends and literal translations that have different meanings
rules.notes: For English 'Abort'

rules.description: Translate 'Actually' correctly
Translate 'Assess' correctly
Translate 'Assume' correctly
Translate 'Billion' correctly
Translate 'Consistent' correctly
Translate 'Eventual' correctly
Translate 'Fabric' correctly
Translate 'Facility' correctly
Translate 'Resume' correctly
Translate 'Support' correctly
Use capital letter for proper names, product names, geographical names
Use capital letter for first word after period, exclamation mark, question mark, ellipsis
rules.notes: When sentence after ellipsis is different or repetition of previous sentence

rules.description: Use capital letter after colon only for quotes, explanatory sentences, or notices/warnings
Use title case for nouns and adjectives of organizations, bodies, departments, awards, events
Use lowercase for nationalities, languages, religions, days of week, months, seasons, currencies, job titles
rules.notes: Contrary to English

rules.description: Use lowercase for months
rules.example_correct: Estará listo a partir de julio.
rules.example_incorrect: Estará listo a partir de Julio.

rules.description: Use lowercase for currencies
Use lowercase for languages
Use lowercase for nationalities
Don't capitalize web and email addresses unless needed for clarity
In titles, headings, UI options, only capitalize first word
Capitalize only first word in terms and conditions titles
Capitalize only first word in page layout references
Capitalize only first word in model names
Retain capitalization in legal texts where it conveys specific meaning
Preserve capitalization for Software in legal contexts
rules.example_correct: Instalar y usar una copia del Software
rules.example_incorrect: Instalar y usar una copia del software

rules.description: Preserve all upper case formatting in paragraphs with legal implications
rules.notes: Sometimes whole paragraphs are written in upper case and formatting must be preserved

rules.description: Run spell-checker when translation is completed
rules.notes: Also recommended to copy text to Word document and run Spelling & Grammar (F7)

rules.description: Separate brand name tags from preceding or following text with a space
No space before any punctuation sign in Spanish
Replace double spaces after full stops with single spaces
rules.notes: English documents often include double spaces after full stops

rules.description: Use non-breaking spaces for names, numbers, software names and versions
rules.notes: Ctrl + Shift + Space in MS Word or Alt+0160 to prevent separation at end of line

rules.description: Separate enumeration elements with comma, last element preceded by conjunction without comma
Don't separate subject from predicate with comma
Always precede and/or follow vocative particle with comma
Place period outside brackets or quotes when sentence is between them
Don't use full stops after headings, titles, table columns, or captions unless full sentence
Don't use full stops if sentence ends with abbreviation, interrogation/exclamation mark, ellipsis, or colon
Avoid using colons as English makes wider use of them, prefer commas where possible
Use colon to introduce enumerations, explanations, notices, advertisements, warnings
Use colon to introduce list of items
Use colon to separate clauses where second expands or illustrates first
Use colon to indicate proportion between numbers
Use colon to separate hours from minutes in numerical time
Start with uppercase after headings like Advertencia, Nota when statement is full sentence
Use semi-colons less than in English, mainly to separate enumeration items when commas would be confusing
Avoid semi-colons and start new sentence instead
Avoid exclamation marks unless really necessary, emphasize main unit rather than whole sentence
rules.notes: Not as common in Spanish as in English

rules.description: Keep only one exclamation mark when they appear in groups
Always use initial and final exclamation marks

rules.section: Use of punctuation - Hyphen/En dash/Em dash
rules.description: Use hyphen to join two parts of compound term

rules.section: Use of punctuation - Hyphen/En dash/Em dash
rules.description: Use en dash as minus sign or in number ranges without spaces

rules.section: Use of punctuation - Hyphen/En dash/Em dash
rules.description: Use em dash to separate element within sentence, always close with final em dash
rules.notes: Spaces around but not inside. Avoid when can be replaced by brackets

rules.section: Use of punctuation - Round and square brackets
rules.description: Use square brackets only when round brackets already appear in sentence
rules.notes: Round brackets commonly seen in Spanish. Square brackets used to define tags

rules.description: Never use straight single quote for variables
rules.notes: Updated 20241207

rules.description: Use quotation marks to quote sentences from other sources or document titles
Avoid single quotes unless software requirements force their use
rules.notes: To avoid breaking builds or technical issues

rules.description: Place punctuation outside quotes
Don't use quotation marks for emphasis, remarks, or DNT terms
Use bar between words to indicate multiple options without spaces
Avoid using ampersand except in trademarked or product names
rules.notes: Notable exceptions: AT&T, H&R Block

rules.description: Follow standard Spanish rules about accent marks
rules.notes: Accent marks mandatory in capitalized letters or words

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If bulleted items are complete sentences, begin with capital and end with period
rules.example_correct:
• Inicie sesión y diríjase a la página de perfil.
• Haga clic en Preferencias y seleccione la opción Idioma.

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If bulleted items are phrases or single words, don't use period
rules.example_correct:
• Símbolos numéricos
• Símbolos y caracteres alfanuméricos
• Cadenas de valores

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If list items depend syntactically on introductory sentence, use lowercase and semicolons except last item with period

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: Be consistent - structure all list items the same way
rules.notes: All items should be fragments or complete sentences. Match tone, voice, verb tense

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: Don't introduce bullet list with preposition
Meta descriptions have maximum 155 characters including spaces and punctuation
rules.notes: Not shorter than 50 characters

rules.description: Meta titles have maximum 55 characters including spaces and punctuation
Follow title case capitalization/formatting rules for meta content
Separate RingCentral from rest of meta title with horizontal bar
Use en dash to separate keywords/keyword phrases
Avoid abbreviations when possible, use standardized abbreviations when needed
rules.notes: May need to abbreviate in UI options

rules.description: Use standard abbreviation for Código
Use standard abbreviation for Departamento
Use standard abbreviation for Estados Unidos
Use standard abbreviation for Etcétera
Use standard abbreviation for Gran Bretaña
Use standard abbreviation for Máximo
Use standard abbreviation for Mínimo
Use standard abbreviation for Número
Use standard abbreviation for Página
Use standard abbreviation for Por ejemplo
Use standard abbreviation for Teléfono
Use standard abbreviation for Unión Europea
Investigate acronym localizability based on RingCentral project requirements
Don't add 'por sus siglas en inglés' between brackets unless specified
Acronyms should not have plural endings
If translation possible for acronym before expansion, locate translation before English
rules.example_correct: CSV (valores separados por comas, Comma Separated Values)

rules.description: If translation not possible for acronym before expansion, leave expansion in parenthesis
If expansion comes before acronym and translation possible, add English expansion in parenthesis after acronym
rules.example_correct: Revoluciones por minuto (RPM, revolution per minute)

rules.description: If expansion comes before acronym and translation not possible, leave expansion and acronym in parenthesis
If acronym not followed by expansion, use acronym without expansion
Write numbers in full when used as nouns
Write numbers in full at beginning of sentence
Write numbers in full when appearing alone representing simple quantities up to 9
Write numbers in full in expressions like 'in the 80s'
Use numerals for dates, large numbers, ages, document references
Replace decimal point with comma
Replace thousand separator with non-breaking space for numbers with 5+ digits
Use comma for decimal separator in 4-digit numbers
Use non-breaking spaces for large number separation
Avoid mixing numerical and alphabetical forms
Separate units of measure from preceding number with non-breaking space
Convert imperial units to metric based on project requirements
rules.notes: May add converted value first with original in brackets or convert to metric equivalent

rules.description: Monitor sizes always expressed in inches as exception
Write percentages in figures followed by % symbol without space
rules.notes: No blank spaces between figure and symbol to avoid confusion

rules.description: Use day-month format not month-day format
rules.notes: Adapt to Spanish standards

rules.description: Use DD/MM/YYYY for short date format
rules.notes: Can be separated by hyphen or bar

rules.description: Use dddd de mmmmm de yyyy for long date format
Use hh:mm:ss for standard time format with 24-hour clock
